claude.question: What are the most recent developments and media coverage surrounding Rick Caruso's involvement in the USC George Tyndall scandal and the unreleased O'Melveny & Myers investigation report?
claude.answer:
Based on my web search findings, here is a comprehensive overview of the most recent developments and media coverage surrounding Rick Caruso's involvement in the USC George Tyndall scandal and the O'Melveny & Myers investigation:

## The Unreleased O'Melveny & Myers Report: Broken Promises and Ongoing Controversy

USC hired the law firm O'Melveny & Myers to conduct an internal investigation into Tyndall and USC's administration to uncover who knew about Tyndall's activities. Caruso promised to make the findings of the investigation publicly available. This did not happen. This broken promise has remained a central point of contention years after the scandal initially emerged. Caruso told reporters in 2018 and 2019 that he planned to make the firm's findings public. To this day, the conclusions remain confidential.

When Caruso's deposition testimony became public in October 2022, it revealed critical details about why the report was never released. Caruso also says that an actual written report by the firm was never presented. The USC board of trustees received a "slide presentation." Furthermore, after O'Melveny's lawyers laid out their conclusions in private presentations to him and other trustees, USC's legal team advised the board not to release the information. When confronted during his deposition, Caruso conceded that he wasn't able to fulfill his "promise of full transparency the way [he] had hoped and intended to."

## Caruso's Justifications and Legal Restrictions

Caruso has offered multiple justifications for withholding the investigation findings. Caruso countered that the decision was based on concern about victims. Experts, he has said, told the trustees the details could retraumatize Tyndall's patients. However, his testimony suggested a different explanation. When pressed during his deposition, with more than half a dozen university lawyers monitoring his testimony, he invoked attorney-client privilege again and again in declining to reveal discussions with administrators about Dr. George Tyndall or the conclusions of an investigation into the gynecologist's troubled history at a campus clinic. "Based on the advice of counsel, as we've talked about, I can't release the information. I'm sorry," Caruso replied.

## Political Controversy and Victim Advocacy Criticism

Caruso's failure to deliver on that promise has been a point of attack for Bass, who this month asked in a radio debate, "What about the victims? If that's what they wanted, they should have had it." During the 2022 Los Angeles mayoral race, his opponent Karen Bass weaponized this issue, arguing that Caruso had failed to prioritize transparency and accountability to victims of abuse. According to the Times, Lawyers from the O'Melveny & Myers law firm engaged in some of the most controversial lines of questioning in the entire testimony. Though Caruso promised reporters in 2018 and 2019 to release these questions to the public, he has yet to do so.

## Current Status and Board Position

As of May 2026, in 2018, Caruso was elected chairman of USC's board of trustees; he resigned from the position in 2022. As of May 2026, he remains a voting member of the board. The O'Melveny & Myers investigation report remains unreleased, and there is no indication from recent media coverage that Caruso intends to make the findings public. The controversy continues to reflect broader concerns about institutional transparency, victim protection, and the tension between legal privilege and public accountability in addressing campus sexual abuse scandals.

claude.question: How has Rick Caruso's track record in real estate development been covered in professional industry publications, and what are the major projects attributed to his company's portfolio?
claude.answer:
Rick Caruso's track record in real estate development has been widely recognized and favorably covered in major industry publications. He was named by the Los Angeles Business Journal as "Developer of the Year" and its 2012 Ernst & Young LLP Master Entrepreneur of the Year. He has been a guest panelist for the International Council of Shopping Centers, a leading trade organization in the retail sector. His prominence in the industry is further reflected in his engagement with academia; Caruso has lectured on real estate issues at the Kennedy School of Public Administration at Harvard University, the USC Price School of Public Policy and the Milken Institute Global Conference.

The financial performance of Caruso's properties has been particularly noteworthy in professional assessments. According to Green Street Advisors, Caruso properties consistently outperform traditional shopping destinations based on productivity and sales per square foot, with results three times higher than the industry average. Both The Grove and The Americana at Brand, according to Forbes, are among the 15 largest in the U.S., in terms of sales per square foot. The Grove and The Americana at Brand have achieved the distinction of being ranked amongst the top ten highest sales-performing shopping centers in the world. Beyond financial metrics, under Rick Caruso's leadership, the company has achieved a compounded annual growth rate of 17% since its inception.

Caruso's major portfolio projects span Southern California and represent his evolution from traditional retail to innovative mixed-use development. His projects include The Grove at Farmers Market in Los Angeles, the Americana at Brand in Glendale, the Commons at Calabasas, the Promenade at Westlake, The Lakes at Thousands Oaks (leased from the City of Thousand Oaks), Waterside Marina del Rey, Palisades Village in Pacific Palisades and the Rosewood Miramar Beach in Santa Barbara. Ignoring the traditional "shopping mall" approach to retail, he adopted a fresh, new strategy for development and revolutionized the industry with imaginative retail and mixed-use destinations that serve as timeless and beloved centers of town.

The Americana at Brand in Glendale, opened in 2002, was designed as a mixed-use development, combining residential, retail, and office space with green parks, fountains, and pedestrian-friendly walkways. The Americana quickly became one of the most popular shopping destinations in the country, consistently ranking among the top retail centers in terms of sales per square foot. More recently, his Palisades Village project, which opened in 2018, is another testament to his ability to blend luxury retail with community-oriented spaces.

Going forward, Caruso's portfolio is currently 80% retail and restaurants and 12% residential but by 2030 is expected to be 19% residential. Caruso, formerly Caruso Affiliated Holdings, LLC, is one of the largest privately held real estate companies in the United States, founded by Rick Caruso in 1987, with Corinne Verdery as its CEO since 2022. The company's continued growth and expansion demonstrates the lasting impact of Caruso's development philosophy in the real estate industry.

claude.question: What public statements and media reactions have emerged following Rick Caruso's announcement to reconsider running for political office in February 2026, particularly in relation to the Palisades Fire crisis?
claude.answer:
## Rick Caruso's February 2026 Political Reconsideration and Media Response

In early February 2026, Rick Caruso temporarily reversed his previous decision to exit electoral politics following a bombshell Los Angeles Times report regarding the Palisades Fire crisis. A bombshell Los Angeles Times report alleging Mayor Karen Bass influenced the Palisades Fire after-action report intensified political fallout across Los Angeles. The LA Times reported that the final report watered down accounts of the city's failure to pre-deploy resources before the January 2025 Palisades Fire, fearing it could expose the city to legal liabilities. This revelation prompted Caruso to reconsider his political aspirations, which he had publicly abandoned just weeks earlier.

Businessman Rick Caruso said the report and its allegations prompted him to reconsider a potential run for mayor. In his public statements, Caruso articulated a sharp distinction between administrative incompetence and alleged cover-up behavior. He stated, "Because incompetence is one thing. But it's very different to mix incompetence, and now you've got somebody who is actively lying to the people that she has sworn to serve." Caruso further characterized the situation as a fundamental breach of governmental responsibility, arguing that any effort to soften an after-action review puts residents at risk and "violates the sacred duty that an elected official has to the residents."

The media response to Caruso's reconsideration was immediate and highlighted the political upheaval caused by the fire report allegations. The bombshell Los Angeles Times report alleging Mayor Karen Bass influenced the Palisades Fire after-action report intensified political fallout across Los Angeles. Local residents in the affected community expressed frustration, with fire survivors in Pacific Palisades surrounded by empty, burned-out lots saying the latest allegations deepened their frustration, with residents stating "It's very disappointing" and "We need the truth." Mayor Bass's office strongly contested the allegations, with "This is muckraking journalism at its lowest form," the mayor's office said in a statement, calling it "dangerous and irresponsible" for reporters to rely on "third hand unsourced information."

However, Caruso's reconsideration proved to be short-lived. Though he said Wednesday he was reconsidering that decision in the wake of the Times report, he ultimately decided to stay out of the race. A Caruso spokesperson said in an emailed message Thursday, a day after Caruso said he was considering entering the race, that "Rick is incredibly moved by the outpouring of support but reached an earlier decision in a thoughtful process and it stands," stating "He will not be a candidate for mayor." Despite his brief flirtation with re-entry into the mayoral race, Caruso's ultimate decision to remain outside electoral politics reflected his conclusion that he could exercise greater impact through philanthropic and civic engagement efforts outside of formal political office.

claude.question: What legal challenges and CEQA-related litigation has Rick Caruso's Caruso company been involved in, and how have these been reported by local and business media outlets?
claude.answer:
Based on my research, Rick Caruso's company has been involved in several notable legal challenges and CEQA-related litigation that have attracted significant attention from local and business media outlets. Here is a detailed overview:

## The Television City CEQA Lawsuit (2025)

The most recent and prominent legal challenge involves The Grove, owned by Caruso in L.A.'s Fairfax District, filing a lawsuit against the city of L.A. and Hackman Capital Partners, claiming that the city's approval of the neighboring Television City studio expansion project violated the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA). The suit ultimately seeks to overturn the city's decision.

The complaint argued that the 25-acre, nearly 1 million-square-foot redevelopment is "frustratingly undefined," making it difficult to accurately measure the project's environmental effects, with the City Council showing "stunning disregard" for CEQA regulations in its approval. The lawsuit alleges that the city violated not just CEQA, but also state housing law and Measure HLA, which requires bus and bike lanes on certain corridors.

This litigation attracted widespread coverage from prominent business and local media outlets including The Hollywood Reporter, Commercial Observer, Bisnow, and The Real Deal. Notably, the case has generated significant irony in coverage, as Caruso had previously maligned CEQA, and as part of his 2022 mayoral campaign, Caruso pledged to require a $15,000 application fee for CEQA complaints to "reduce frivolous CEQA challenges that impede sensible development". In 2022, the Grove filed a 374-page comment opposing Television City's draft environmental review, even as Caruso, running for mayor, publicly claimed that he supported the project.

## Historical CEQA and Environmental Litigation

Caruso's company has faced CEQA challenges on other projects as well. For The Shops at Santa Anita in Arcadia, Westfield LLC filed a lawsuit on the Caruso development's environmental-impact report (EIR), alleging that Caruso's EIR, which was approved by Arcadia's City Council on April 17, 2007, did not tell the public the entire story of what Caruso plans to do with the project.

## Carlsbad Initiative and CEQA Circumvention

In 2015, Caruso funded a voter initiative to bypass local planning laws and CEQA in order to build an outdoor mall in Carlsbad, California. Although the initiative passed, a subsequent referendum overturned it and a public vote was required, with the Measure A ultimately failing and the mall construction being blocked.

## Competitive Legal Battles

Beyond CEQA matters, Caruso's company has been involved in significant competitive litigation. According to the developer, he spent $20 million to fight lawsuits and political measures so he could build The Americana at Brand, with General Growth Properties Inc. suing Caruso and waging a political campaign to stop The Americana. In November 2007, a Superior Court jury ordered the owner of the Glendale Galleria to pay $15 million in punitive damages to Caruso.

## Media Characterization

The recent Television City lawsuit has drawn particularly sharp commentary from local outlets. Media coverage has highlighted the apparent contradiction between Caruso's public stance on development and his use of CEQA challenges to block a neighboring project, with coverage suggesting this represents a strategic approach to protecting his existing properties rather than principled environmental concerns.

claude.question: How have political analysts and media outlets characterized Rick Caruso's multiple party affiliation changes between 2011-2022, and what explanations has he provided for these shifts?
claude.answer:
## Rick Caruso's Party Affiliation Changes: Political Analysis and Criticism

Rick Caruso's multiple party affiliation shifts between 2011 and 2022 have drawn substantial scrutiny from political analysts and media outlets, who have characterized the moves with skepticism and attributed them primarily to political opportunism rather than genuine ideological evolution.

### The Timeline of Party Changes

Caruso, who was a Republican for much of his life, switched his party registration to "decline to state" in 2011 while mulling over a run in the city's 2013 mayoral election. In March 2016, he re-registered as a Republican, and he kept his Republican Party affiliation until mid-November 2019, when he changed his registration back to "no party preference." Caruso changed his party affiliation to Democratic in late January of this year, less than a month before filing to run in the 2022 mayoral election. This pattern represents four changes in party registration over the last 11 years.

### Media and Political Characterization

Political analysts and opponents have been critical of Caruso's registration history. Darry Sragow, a veteran Democratic strategist and publisher of the nonpartisan California Target Book, thought that Caruso's repeated changes in party affiliation could raise questions for voters, suggesting that frequent changes in voter registration could indicate that "the candidate really doesn't have a core" or that they are an opportunist. Media coverage has similarly portrayed his shifts as inconsistent and opaque. Caruso has typically dismissed criticism and most questions about his registration history, and voters seeking to better understand his zigzagging political transformation found few answers in his $25-million barrage of advertisements.

### Democratic Opposition and Accusations

The most forceful criticism came from Democratic leaders when Caruso switched to the Democratic Party in advance of the 2022 mayoral race. Karen Bass stated: "You can't just change your party registration, change your viewpoints and spend tens of millions of dollars trying to convince people that you're somebody you're not. If you lie about who you are, if you lie about who other people are, how could you possibly bring the city together to meet the needs that we have in Los Angeles right now?" Steve Kang, president of the Korean American Democratic Committee, asserted: "He's been a Republican nearly his entire life, supporting far right extremists worth millions of dollars. He's only been a Democrat in the last year, and he did it so that he could trick voters into thinking he's something that he is not." Political opponents also tied his party switches to his donation history. Fellow mayoral candidate and L.A. City Councilmember Kevin de León said: "His track record speaks for itself: from serving as Trump's economic advisor, to backing antiabortion and pro-NRA politicians like Mitch McConnell."

### Caruso's Explanation and Response

Caruso has offered limited explanation for his party switches and has dismissed criticism about them. He said he became an independent because he didn't like ideology. When questioned about his registration changes during his 2022 campaign, Caruso stated: "I'm a Democrat, and I'm proud to be a Democrat. What fascinates me is that we're even having this conversation because the electorate, the voters in the city don't care about this." Rather than engaging substantively with questions about his political evolution, Caruso has typically dismissed criticism and most questions about his registration history. Political observers have noted that there is strategic value in switching party preference, as it allows endorsements that may have been previously difficult to come by.

claude.question: What has been the public and media narrative around Rick Caruso's $100+ million personal spending on his 2022 Los Angeles mayoral campaign and its defeat to Karen Bass?
claude.answer:
## Rick Caruso's $100+ Million Campaign Spending and Defeat: The Public and Media Narrative

The public and media narrative surrounding Rick Caruso's massive campaign expenditure in the 2022 Los Angeles mayoral race has centered on the dramatic disparity between his spending and electoral results, raising fundamental questions about the relationship between wealth and political success in American elections.

### The Staggering Financial Imbalance

Caruso spent $104,848,887.43 on the mayor's race through November 2, while Bass spent $9,060,958.59, creating an unprecedented spending gap in Los Angeles electoral history. Bass was outspent by around 11 to 1, with her billionaire opponent breaking local campaign spending records. The efficiency metrics proved particularly striking and became a focal point for media analysis. Caruso spent $160.42 per vote while Karen Bass spent $10.15 to become L.A. mayor. When accounting for the full campaign cycle, Caruso's total per vote spent comes to $338.42, for Bass, the total is $21.94.

### The Failure of Money to Guarantee Victory

Despite his record-breaking expenditures and late campaign surge, after a close initial count on election night, the late returns broke Bass' way, and she ended up beating him by nearly 10 percentage points, or almost 90,000 votes. The media narrative emphasized that his massive spending, which included an aggressive advertising blitz, ultimately failed to translate into electoral success. After spending nearly $100 million in the Los Angeles mayoral campaign, Rick Caruso has rapidly caught up to his rival Karen Bass, putting the race in a statistical tie in late polling, yet he still lost decisively in the actual election.

### Questions About Campaign Effectiveness

The narrative also grappled with whether money effectively moved voters. It's unclear whether the flood of dollars moves the needle effectively. In the primary, Caruso ended up spending an average of $176 for every vote he received. Political analysts noted that while Caruso's money has been well spent in terms of persuasion, with negative ads having their effect and more people having an unfavorable opinion of Karen Bass than in the past, the number of undecided voters dwindled and seemingly more made decisions for Caruso than for Bass. However, this advantage in persuasion metrics did not translate into victory on Election Day.

### The Broader Context and Legacy

The media coverage contextualized Caruso's loss within broader discussions about campaign finance and democratic representation. Los Angeles might not have another candidate willing to dump $100 million of their own money into the race the next time the mayor's office is up for grabs. The narrative also drew comparisons to Michael Bloomberg's successful 2009 New York mayoral campaign, with Caruso not being the first person in the country to spend more than $100 million on a mayor's race, as New York City Mayor Michael Bloomberg dispensed $102 million of his own money on his successful 2009 bid for a third term, which pencils out at roughly $183 per vote. This comparison highlighted that even billionaire spending does not guarantee victory, particularly in liberal-leaning cities.

claude.question: What philanthropic contributions, community initiatives, or social impact programs associated with Rick Caruso or his company have been documented in public records or news coverage?
claude.answer:
## Rick Caruso's Philanthropic Contributions and Community Initiatives

Rick Caruso has established a substantial philanthropic legacy spanning multiple decades, with documented contributions to education, healthcare, and community development throughout Southern California. His charitable work is primarily channeled through the Caruso Family Foundation and direct personal donations to institutions of higher education and community organizations.

### Higher Education and Student Support

In 2019, Caruso committed $50 million to the Pepperdine School of Law, inspiring the new name of Rick J. Caruso School of Law in recognition of the historic gift. The contribution, made through the Caruso Family Foundation, will expand educational access to exceptional, historically underserved student populations—a hallmark of the Caruso family's philanthropic efforts in the Los Angeles area and beyond—as well as fuel several initiatives to strengthen the School of Law's academic programming. Caruso also established the Caruso Family Loan Forgiveness Fund, an endowed fund for graduates who are dedicated to a career in public service.

Beyond Pepperdine, Caruso has made significant contributions to his alma mater. With a recent pledge of renewed support for the USC Caruso Catholic Center, his family donated the lead gift to build the center and Our Savior Parish Church more than a decade ago, the Caruso family's total giving to the university now exceeds $50 million. Those values prompted Rick and Tina Caruso to endow the USC Tina and Rick Caruso Department of OtolaryngologyHead and Neck Surgery with a $25 million gift in 2015. This medical department focus was inspired by personal experience: They became personally invested in advancing the field when their daughter, Gianna, was born with moderate hearing loss.

### Community Youth and Education Programs

Founded in 1991, the Caruso Family Foundation is dedicated to improving the lives of at-risk children and their families throughout Southern California. The foundation supports organizations with a proven track record of providing exceptional services and programs to the communities they serve. A primary focus has been on underserved neighborhoods. In 2013, the Caruso Family Foundation made a $5 million commitment to launch the Operation Progress ecosystem in the Watts neighborhood of Los Angeles, an area that has among the lowest life expectancy and college graduation rates in California.

Tina and Rick Caruso have donated more than $3 million and established the Tina & Rick Caruso Early Education Center, providing Early Head Start and Head Start for 80+ infants and toddlers 6 weeks old to 5 years old, in the heart of Skid Row. This center is operated through Para Los Niños, an organization that Caruso has supported for decades.

### Employee Volunteerism and Community Service

Caruso's philanthropic philosophy extends to corporate-level engagement. In addition to many of our employees doubling as mentors through Operation Progress and Caruso Scholar programs, we have been lucky to work with local organizations to prepare and feed homeless veterans, work on construction projects at local faith-based schools, and support our city's first responders. Born from Caruso's commitment to supporting causes that positively impact the city of Los Angeles, A Day of Service is a dedicated day in which Caruso's corporate offices close and employees commit time to philanthropic organizations across Los Angeles.

### Emergency Response and Recent Initiatives

More recently, Caruso established Steadfast LA in response to natural disasters. Steadfast LA is a civic nonprofit, founded by Rick Caruso and led by the private sector, dedicated to helping Los Angeles rebuild faster, stronger, and more resilient after disaster. In the context of the January 2025 Southern California wildfires, Caruso and his wife pledged $5 million to the Los Angeles Fire Department Foundation. In the aftermath of the devastating wildfires in Los Angeles in early 2025, he and Tina gave a $5 million lead gift to raise $20 million for critical resources for Los Angeles Fire Department. This $20 million goal was met in 6 days.

### Recognition and Legacy

Recently, he was named by Town and Country magazine as one of the top 50 philanthropists in the nation. He was also honored by Pope Francis with the rank of Commander in the Order of St. Gregory the Great for his service and generosity to so many in need. These recognized contributions demonstrate that Caruso's philanthropic work has extended well beyond his real estate business, with documented commitments totaling hundreds of millions of dollars focused primarily on educational access and support for historically underserved populations in Southern California.
